body.title: agent_datacenter: YGM/Nudge pipeline — ygm and STOP injection only, minimal footprint
body.result: ygm_check.py + ClaudeShim hook registration shipped; JSONL fallback during dual-write window
body.description:
Implement the YGM (You've Got Mail) Nudge pipeline. On every query submit, check Claude's IMAP mailbox for UNSEEN messages and inject one of exactly two tokens: 'ygm' (you have mail) or 'STOP' (halt signal). Minimal footprint by design.

Decision (Akien 2026-04-27): Only two injectable tokens. ygm = 'you have N unread messages from: {from_list}'. STOP = halt signal. Full message bodies stay in mailbox; Claude reads them explicitly via tool call. This keeps injection minimal and footprint small.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/devices/inference/nudge.py:
  class NudgePipeline:
    def check_and_inject(self, mailbox: str, query_context: str) -> str:
      unseen = imap_server.unseen_count(mailbox)
      if unseen == 0: return query_context  # clean path, no injection
      messages = imap_server.fetch_unseen(mailbox)  # marks as SEEN
      stop_messages = [m for m in messages if m.payload.get('type') == 'STOP']
      if stop_messages: return query_context + '\n\nSTOP'  # halt signal injected
      from_list = [m.from_device for m in messages]
      return query_context + f'\n\nygm ({unseen} unread, from: {from_list})'  # ygm injected
    Hook: fires on query submit, before context reaches model
    Mid-stream messages: wait until next query (no interrupt)
    Only two output forms: 'ygm (N unread, from: [...])' or 'STOP'

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/inference/nudge.py
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/inference/device.py (integrate hook)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: NudgePipeline docstring states 'only ygm and STOP are valid injection types — minimal footprint by design'

Scope boundary:
IN: IMAP UNSEEN check, ygm injection with count+from, STOP injection, mark SEEN
OUT: full message body injection (bodies stay in mailbox; Claude reads via tool call), v2 format (future ticket)

Test plan:
- No unseen → query_context returned unchanged (clean path)
- 3 unseen from ['igor', 'web-server', 'igor'] → 'ygm (3 unread, from: [...])' injected
- STOP message in mailbox → 'STOP' injected (even if other unread messages exist)
- After check_and_inject(): unseen_count == 0 (all marked SEEN)
